stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Roughly 89% of Madagascar’s estimated 11,000 plant species are flowering plants.
  • Flowering plants of the family Orchidaceae, which includes ground orchids and epiphytic orchids, are estimated to total 1,000 species in Madagascar.
  • Flowering plants of the family Fabaceae, which includes legumes and acacias, are estimated to total 700 species in Madagascar.
  • Some of these flowering plants’ populations are threatened by deforestation and invasive insect species.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the different families into which Madagascar’s flowering plants are classified. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The family Orchidaceae is estimated to make up 1,000 of Madagascar’s 11,000 plant species.
Many ground orchids and epiphytic orchids of the family Orchidaceae are threatened by deforestation in Madagascar.
Among Madagascar’s many flowering plants, ground orchids and epiphytic orchids belong to the family Orchidaceae, while legumes and acacias belong to the family Fabaceae.
In Madagascar, 700 species are estimated to be legumes and acacias, both classified as flowering plants of the family Fabaceae.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It names both families and explicitly contrasts the plant groups in each, emphasizing the different family classifications.

Choices A, B, and D each mention only one family, failing to emphasize the difference between the two families.
